title: The Sixth Sunday of Easter
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## May 22, 2022 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

LITURGICAL CONTEXT

The Sixth Sunday of Easter falls seven weeks after Easter Sunday (April 10, 2022), placing this sermon in the Eastertide season leading toward Pentecost (June 5, 2022). The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday typically include Acts 16:9-15 (Paul's vision of the Macedonian), Psalm 67, Revelation 21:10, 22-26, and John 14:23-29 (Jesus's promise of the Holy Spirit). This period emphasizes the risen Christ's post-resurrection appearances and the imminent gift of the Holy Spirit, with themes of witness and preparation for Pentecost.

EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating ongoing debates about human sexuality and LGBTQ+ inclusion in the lead-up to the 79th General Convention scheduled for July 2022 in Baltimore, where major decisions on same-sex marriage and transgender participation were expected. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Gina Campbell Brewer (who had served since 2019), was engaged in standard spring parish activities. Saint John's Cathedral itself, located in the Cathedral Chapter, was the seat of diocesan authority and likely housed visiting clergy and canonical functions typical of the pre-summer church calendar.

DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver in late May 2022 was experiencing unusual heat with temperatures in the 80s and approaching 90s by mid-month, unusual for late spring in the high plains. The city was in full spring mode with outdoor events resuming post-pandemic; the Denver Botanic Gardens and other cultural institutions were hosting seasonal programming. No major local political crises or weather emergencies dominated local news in the immediate week before May 22, though water and drought concerns remained chronic issues for Colorado.

NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Uvalde school shooting in Texas occurred on May 24, 2022 (two days after this sermon), shocking the nation with 19 children and 2 teachers killed, though Lawson's sermon preceded this tragedy and would not reflect it. President Biden was managing ongoing concerns about inflation, which had reached 8.6% year-over-year, the highest in 40 years, and gasoline prices averaged $5 per gallon nationally. The January 6 Committee was conducting high-profile hearings leading up to televised prime-time sessions beginning June 9, 2022, keeping political divisions at the forefront of national consciousness.

INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Ukraine-Russia war continued intensely through May 2022, with Russia consolidating territorial gains in eastern Ukraine and the siege of Mariupol having concluded weeks prior, creating an ongoing humanitarian crisis. The cost-of-living crisis was spreading across Europe and globally, with inflation spiking in the UK, EU, and other Western economies, partly driven by Russian energy embargoes and supply chain disruptions. China's zero-COVID lockdowns in Shanghai and other major cities were producing economic and social strain visible worldwide.

CULTURAL MOMENT

The national mood in late May 2022 was characterized by anxiety about inflation, gun violence concerns building toward the Uvalde tragedy, and exhaustion over prolonged political polarization and Ukraine war coverage. Popular culture offered escapism with the return of summer entertainment; the Marvel film "Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness" had just released and "Top Gun: Maverick" was dominating box offices. Denver's cultural life was reopening fully with Elitch Gardens amusement park operating at full capacity and the Denver Zoo hosting normal spring attendance, offering a sense of normalization after pandemic years, creating a tension between local recovery and national anxiety.
